description: Posts that prominently feature restaurant name, location, operating hours, and establishment credentials (awards, origins) to establish legitimacy and accessibility for potential customers.
structural_recipe: Lead with restaurant name in Arabic and English. Include precise location with address or recognizable landmark (📍 emoji + area name). List operating hours clearly. Add credibility markers: Michelin stars, chef origin story, culinary accolades, or international prestige. Use structured formatting with line breaks and descriptive tags (@tags for similar restaurants). Balance formal information with approachable tone.
why_it_works: Saudi diners prioritize convenience, authenticity verification, and aspirational dining. Displaying operational details removes friction from the decision-making process. Michelin stars and international credentials resonate with Saudi luxury consumers' preference for globally-recognized excellence. Clear location/hours signals professionalism and respect for customer time.
transplantation_caveats: Ensure all operational details (hours, location) are 100% accurate—misinformation damages trust in Saudi market
Verify award claims (Michelin stars, ratings) before posting—false credentials are heavily scrutinized
Adapt location descriptions to Saudi geography familiarity (use Via names, mall names, well-known neighborhoods)
Arabic/English bilingual formatting is expected in Saudi F&B; monolingual reduces credibility
cultural_constraints: Must include prayer/halal credibility if applicable to target audience expectations
International prestige (London origins) leverages aspirational values but must be paired with local accessibility
Formal, detailed information structure appeals to Gulf business culture preference for transparency
